hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance complementarity with SSTR2-specific ECL2/TM5 contacts, while mutating position 11 to hydrophobic residues (L/I/V) will reduce off-target SSTR3/SSTR5 binding without compromising SSTR2 affinity. This will increase the selectivity margin (Δmargin) by improving SSTR2-specific interactions.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current hypothesis remains valid: strong SSTR2 binding is not sufficient; selectivity over SSTR3/SSTR5 is the limiting factor. The next step is to refine the sequence to disrupt off-target interactions at non-pharmacophore positions.

proposed_changes.rationale: To improve selectivity, we must disrupt conserved pocket contacts in off-targets SSTR3/SSTR5. Focusing on non-pharmacophore positions 1/2/5/6/11/12 allows us to tune interactions without compromising the pharmacophore FWKT(7-10) or Cys3/Cys14.

These mutations are designed to disrupt conserved pocket interactions in SSTR3/SSTR5. Positively charged residues at positions 1 and 2 can reduce off-target binding, while polar residues at 5, 6, 11, and 12 can break hydrophobic interactions.
